---
title: "Updating genomes and indices"
author: "AY"
editor: source
date: last-modified
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 4
    toc-expand: false
    number-sections: true
    number-depth: 4
---

This page is the operator guide for `./scripts/bcfngs genomes`.
It covers:

- checking whether the pinned genome release is outdated
- planning and building genome sources plus indices
- rebuilding only selected organisms
- updating genome configuration and versioned index directories
- advanced direct `snakemake` usage when the wrapper does not expose a knob

Environment setup is documented elsewhere. This page assumes `./scripts/bcfngs` already runs on your machine or cluster.

## Command overview

`bcfngs genomes` currently exposes three subcommands:

- `check-updates`: compare the pinned `releases.ensembl` value in [`configs/genomes.yaml`](configs/genomes.yaml) with the latest release advertised on `ftp.ensembl.org`
- `plan`: validate the genomes config, check remote FASTA/GTF URLs, verify write access to `genomes_root`, lint the workflow, and do a dry-run
- `build`: execute the genomes workflow for real; by default it skips `plan`, and `--no-skip-plan` enables the preflight checks before the build

During `build`, the wrapper also auto-detects the active `STAR`, `bwa`, and `bowtie2` versions from the environment and uses those detected versions for the versioned output directories.

## Shared wrapper parameters

These apply to all three genomes subcommands and are cross-referenced from the chapters below.

| Parameter | Default | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `--profile` | `micromamba` | `modules` prepends `module load bclconvert/4.3.13 star bowtie2 bwa`, but still runs `python` and `snakemake` through micromamba. |
| `--env-prefix` | `~/miniconda3/envs/update_genomes` if present, otherwise `./.mamba/env` | Shown here with `~` for brevity. The current logic in [`scripts/bcfngs`](scripts/bcfngs) prefers the cluster micromamba environment when it exists; otherwise it falls back to the repo-local environment. |

## Common config fields

These are the main `configs/genomes.yaml` fields referenced by the three command chapters.

| Field | Value | Used | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| `genomes_root` | `/fs/pool/pool-bcfngs/genomes` | `plan/build` | `plan` checks write access here. `build` writes sources, indices, logs, chromSize files, and symlinks here. |
| `releases.ensembl` | `115` | `check-updates/plan/build` | Main Ensembl release pin. `check-updates` compares this to `ftp.ensembl.org`. |
| `releases.ensemblgenomes.metazoa` | `62` | `plan/build` | Used for `ensemblgenomes` organisms such as `Drosophila_pseudoobscura`. |
| `default_threads` | `16` | `build` | Used by `STAR` genome generation and `bowtie2-build`. |
| `default_ram_bytes` | `168632718037` | `build` | Passed to `STAR --limitGenomeGenerateRAM`. |
| `bwa_index_algorithm` | `bwtsw` | `build` | Global default for `bwa index -a`. |
| `bwa_index_mem_mb` | `16000` | `build` | Global memory resource for the `bwa_index` rule. |
| `organisms.*` | all configured organisms | `plan/build` | Scientific-name keys such as `Homo_sapiens`, `Mus_musculus`, `Drosophila_melanogaster`. |
| `organisms.*.fasta_kind` / `gtf_kind` | organism-specific | `plan/build` | Used to construct standard Ensembl file names when explicit URLs are not provided. |
| `organisms.*.sa_index` | organism-specific | `build` | Passed to `STAR --genomeSAindexNbases`. |
| `organisms.*.bwa_index_algorithm` | optional override | `build` | Overrides the global BWA algorithm for one organism. |
| `organisms.*.bwa_index_mem_mb` | optional override | `build` | Overrides the global BWA memory resource for one organism. |
| `organisms.*.fasta_url` / `gtf_url` | optional explicit URLs | `plan/build` | Used for organisms that do not fit the generated Ensembl filename pattern. |

## Overview of `configs/genomes.yaml`

At the top level, [`configs/genomes.yaml`](configs/genomes.yaml) is split into:

- `genomes_root`: output root for sources, logs, indices, and symlinks
- `releases`: the pinned upstream releases
- `default_threads` and `default_ram_bytes`: global defaults for the heavy index rules
- `bwa_index_algorithm` and `bwa_index_mem_mb`: global defaults for the BWA index rule
- `organisms`: one block per organism, keyed by scientific name

Typical organism block:

```yaml
Homo_sapiens:
  source: ensembl
  species: homo_sapiens
  assembly: GRCh38
  fasta_kind: dna.primary_assembly
  gtf_kind: gtf
  sa_index: 14
  bwa_index_algorithm: bwtsw
  bwa_index_mem_mb: 64000
```

Fields under `organisms:`:

- the organism key, such as `Homo_sapiens`, is the name you pass to `--organism`
- `source` is usually `ensembl`, and can also be `ensemblgenomes`
- `division` is required for `ensemblgenomes` sources, for example `metazoa`
- `species` is the lowercase species path used on the FTP server
- `assembly` is the assembly label used in the remote FASTA/GTF file names
- `fasta_kind` and `gtf_kind` control the file naming pattern for standard Ensembl sources
- `sa_index` is the STAR suffix-array parameter
- `bwa_index_algorithm` optionally overrides the global BWA algorithm for that organism
- `bwa_index_mem_mb` optionally overrides the global BWA memory resource for that organism
- `fasta_url` and `gtf_url` can be set explicitly when the source naming does not match the default template

### Adding a new genome

1. Add a new organism block under `organisms:` using the scientific name as the key, for example `Arabidopsis_thaliana`.
2. Set `source`, `species`, `assembly`, `fasta_kind`, `gtf_kind`, and `sa_index`.
3. If the default Ensembl path logic does not work, provide explicit `fasta_url` and `gtf_url`.
4. If BWA needs a different algorithm or memory budget for that organism, add `bwa_index_algorithm` and/or `bwa_index_mem_mb`.
5. Run `./scripts/bcfngs genomes plan`.
6. Build only that organism first with `./scripts/bcfngs genomes build --organism <Organism>`.

## `check-updates`

### CLI parameters

| Parameter | Default | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| shared wrapper parameters | see [Shared wrapper parameters](#shared-wrapper-parameters) | `--profile` and `--env-prefix` still apply here. |
| command-specific flags | none | `check-updates` only supports `-h/--help`. |

### Config-file settings

| Field | Value | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `releases.ensembl` | `115` | This is the pinned release compared to the latest Ensembl release found on `https://ftp.ensembl.org/pub/`. |
| not checked automatically | n/a | `releases.ensemblgenomes.*`, explicit `fasta_url`, and explicit `gtf_url` are not audited by this command. |

### Run command

For this subcommand, `--profile micromamba` and `--profile modules` do the same logical work. `check-updates` uses `python` + `PyYAML` and `curl` through the wrapper environment; it does not call `STAR`, `bwa`, or `bowtie2-build`.

```bash
./scripts/bcfngs genomes check-updates
```

Optional equivalent command under the `modules` profile:

```bash
module load bclconvert/4.3.13 star bowtie2 bwa
./scripts/bcfngs --profile modules genomes check-updates
```

Typical output looks like:

```text
Ensembl latest release: <latest>  current pinned: 115  status: OK|UPDATE_AVAILABLE
Suggested bump: set releases.ensembl to <latest> in configs/genomes.yaml
```

### If an update is available

1. Edit [`configs/genomes.yaml`](configs/genomes.yaml).
2. Bump `releases.ensembl`, for example `115` to `116`.
3. Review any organism entries that use explicit `fasta_url` or `gtf_url`.
4. Continue with [`plan`](#plan) and then [`build`](#build).

If you are updating an EnsemblGenomes organism such as `Drosophila_pseudoobscura`, also review `releases.ensemblgenomes` plus the explicit URLs in its organism block.

## `plan`

`plan` has no command-specific CLI parameters. Shared wrapper parameters are listed in [Shared wrapper parameters](#shared-wrapper-parameters).

### Config-file settings

| Field | Value | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `genomes_root` | `/fs/pool/pool-bcfngs/genomes` | `plan` checks that this location is writable. |
| `releases.*` | `ensembl: 115`, `ensemblgenomes.metazoa: 62` | Used to construct remote URLs for all non-explicit organisms. |
| `organisms.*` | all configured organisms | The wrapper plans the full configured set. |
| `organisms.*.fasta_url` / `gtf_url` | optional | Explicit URLs are checked as given. |

### Runtime behavior

| Step | Notes |
|---|---|
| YAML load | `plan` first validates that [`configs/genomes.yaml`](configs/genomes.yaml) can be parsed. |
| URL check | It resolves all FASTA/GTF URLs and tests them with `curl -fsSI`. |
| tool version check | It detects `STAR`, `bwa`, and `bowtie2` versions from the active environment before the dry-run. |
| workflow lint | It runs `snakemake --lint` on [`workflow/genomes.smk`](workflow/genomes.smk). |
| dry-run | It runs a dry-run with the checked-in genomes config. |

### Local run with micromamba / conda-style environment

Use this before every real build:

```bash
./scripts/bcfngs genomes plan
```

This checks that:

- [`configs/genomes.yaml`](configs/genomes.yaml) parses
- `genomes_root` is writable
- each configured FASTA/GTF URL responds
- [`workflow/genomes.smk`](workflow/genomes.smk) passes `snakemake --lint`
- the workflow dry-run resolves successfully

### Local run with modules

If your cluster provides aligners via modules:

```bash
module load bclconvert/4.3.13 star bowtie2 bwa
./scripts/bcfngs --profile modules genomes plan
```

The `modules` profile still uses the micromamba environment for `python` and `snakemake`; it only changes how tool discovery happens for the aligners.

## `build`

### CLI parameters

| Parameter | Default | Used when | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| shared wrapper parameters | see [Shared wrapper parameters](#shared-wrapper-parameters) | all builds | `--profile` and `--env-prefix` apply here too. |
| `--executor` | `local` | all builds | Choose `local` or `slurm`. |
| `--cores` | `16` | `--executor local` | Total Snakemake core budget for local execution. |
| `--jobs` | `20` | `--executor slurm` | Max concurrent jobs for the SLURM executor. |
| `--organism` | all configured organisms | all builds | Optional comma-separated subset such as `Homo_sapiens,Mus_musculus`. |
| `--no-skip-plan` | off | all builds | `build` skips `plan` by default. Set this flag to run the preflight checks first. |
| `--slurm-partition` | `<SLURM_PARTITION>` | `--executor slurm` | Cluster-specific partition name. |
| `--slurm-account` | unset | `--executor slurm` | Optional SLURM account. |
| `--slurm-qos` | unset | `--executor slurm` | Optional SLURM QoS. |
| `--slurm-mail-user` | `<SLURM_MAIL_USER>` | `--executor slurm` | Cluster-specific mail recipient. |
| `--slurm-mail-type` | `ALL` | `--executor slurm` | Mail type passed to SLURM. |
| `--slurm-time` | `04:00:00` | `--executor slurm` | Walltime for submitted jobs. |
| `--slurm-cpus-per-task` | `20` | `--executor slurm` | Applied to heavy index rules. |
| `--slurm-mem-per-cpu` | unset | `--executor slurm` | If set, it overrides `--slurm-mem`. |
| `--slurm-mem` | `409600` | `--executor slurm` | Total memory in MB for heavy index rules. |
| `--slurm-exclusive` | off | `--executor slurm` | Request exclusive node allocation. |

### Config-file settings

| Field | Value | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `genomes_root` | `/fs/pool/pool-bcfngs/genomes` | Build outputs, logs, and symlinks are written here. |
| `releases.*` | `ensembl: 115`, `ensemblgenomes.metazoa: 62` | Used to resolve the download URLs unless explicit URLs are present. |
| `default_threads` | `16` | Used by `STAR` genome generation and `bowtie2-build`. This is separate from `--cores`. |
| `default_ram_bytes` | `168632718037` | Passed to `STAR --limitGenomeGenerateRAM`. |
| `bwa_index_algorithm` | `bwtsw` | Global default for `bwa index -a`. |
| `bwa_index_mem_mb` | `16000` | Global memory resource for `bwa_index`. |
| `organisms.*.sa_index` | organism-specific | Controls STAR suffix-array sizing. |
| `organisms.*.bwa_index_algorithm` | optional per-organism override | Overrides the global BWA algorithm for one organism. |
| `organisms.*.bwa_index_mem_mb` | optional per-organism override | Overrides the global BWA memory resource for one organism. |
| `organisms.*.fasta_url` / `gtf_url` | optional | Explicit URLs override the generated release-based URLs. |

### Runtime behavior

| Runtime value | Notes |
|---|---|
| tool versions | `build` auto-detects the active `STAR`, `bwa`, and `bowtie2` versions from the environment and injects them into a temporary config file for versioned output directories. |
| plan step | `build` does not run `plan` unless `--no-skip-plan` is set. |
| symlink updates | Successful builds refresh `starIndex`, `bwaIndex`, and `bowtie2Index` symlinks to point at the versioned directories. |

### Current build help output

```bash
./scripts/bcfngs genomes build --help
```

```text
usage: bcfngs genomes build [-h] [--executor {local,slurm}] [--cores CORES]
                            [--jobs JOBS] [--slurm-partition SLURM_PARTITION]
                            [--slurm-account SLURM_ACCOUNT]
                            [--slurm-qos SLURM_QOS]
                            [--slurm-mail-user SLURM_MAIL_USER]
                            [--slurm-mail-type SLURM_MAIL_TYPE]
                            [--slurm-time SLURM_TIME]
                            [--slurm-cpus-per-task SLURM_CPUS_PER_TASK]
                            [--slurm-mem-per-cpu SLURM_MEM_PER_CPU]
                            [--slurm-mem SLURM_MEM] [--slurm-exclusive]
                            [--organism ORGANISM] [-no-skip-plan]

optional arguments:
  -h, --help            show this help message and exit
  --executor {local,slurm}
                        Execution backend: local (default) or slurm.
  --cores CORES
  --jobs JOBS           Max concurrent jobs for --executor slurm.
  --slurm-partition SLURM_PARTITION
                        SLURM partition name (for --executor slurm).
  --slurm-account SLURM_ACCOUNT
                        SLURM account name (for --executor slurm).
  --slurm-qos SLURM_QOS
                        SLURM QoS name (for --executor slurm).
  --slurm-mail-user SLURM_MAIL_USER
                        SLURM mail user for --executor slurm.
  --slurm-mail-type SLURM_MAIL_TYPE
                        SLURM mail type for --executor slurm (e.g. ALL or
                        END,FAIL).
  --slurm-time SLURM_TIME
                        SLURM walltime HH:MM:SS (default: 04:00:00).
  --slurm-cpus-per-task SLURM_CPUS_PER_TASK
                        SLURM cpus-per-task for heavy index rules (default:
                        20).
  --slurm-mem-per-cpu SLURM_MEM_PER_CPU
                        SLURM mem-per-cpu in MB for --executor slurm
                        (optional). If set, this overrides --slurm-mem.
  --slurm-mem SLURM_MEM
                        SLURM total memory (--mem) in MB for heavy index rules
                        (default: 409600 ~= 400G). Ignored when --slurm-mem-
                        per-cpu is set.
  --slurm-exclusive     Request exclusive node allocation per submitted SLURM
                        job.
  --organism ORGANISM   Comma-separated subset of organism keys (e.g.
                        Homo_sapiens,Mus_musculus)
  -no-skip-plan, --no-skip-plan
                        Run plan preflight checks before build (config
                        validation, URL checks, lint, dry-run).
```

### Local run with micromamba / conda-style environment

Build all configured genomes:

```bash
./scripts/bcfngs genomes build --cores 20
```

More local examples:

```bash
./scripts/bcfngs genomes build --cores 8
./scripts/bcfngs genomes build --cores 20
./scripts/bcfngs genomes build --cores 32
```

`--cores` is the total Snakemake job budget. It is not the same thing as `default_threads`, which controls per-tool threading inside rules such as `STAR` genome generation.

If you want the build to include the preflight checks:

```bash
./scripts/bcfngs genomes build --cores 20 --no-skip-plan
```

### Local run with modules

```bash
module load bclconvert/4.3.13 star bowtie2 bwa
./scripts/bcfngs --profile modules genomes build --cores 20
```

This is the best fit when the aligners should come from modules but `python` and `snakemake` should still come from the micromamba environment.

### Build only selected organisms

Organism names must match the keys in [`configs/genomes.yaml`](configs/genomes.yaml) exactly.

```bash
./scripts/bcfngs genomes build --cores 20 --organism Homo_sapiens
./scripts/bcfngs genomes build --cores 20 --organism Homo_sapiens,Mus_musculus
./scripts/bcfngs genomes build --cores 12 --organism Drosophila_melanogaster,Drosophila_pseudoobscura
```

Use this when:

- you added or changed only one organism
- you want to test a release bump on a smaller subset first
- you do not want to rebuild every organism after a config edit

### SLURM-oriented build example

Use the wrapper directly for cluster-scheduled builds:

```bash
./scripts/bcfngs genomes build \
  --executor slurm \
  --jobs 20 \
  --slurm-partition <SLURM_PARTITION> \
  --slurm-mem 409600
```

If your site schedules by memory per CPU instead:

```bash
./scripts/bcfngs genomes build \
  --executor slurm \
  --jobs 20 \
  --slurm-cpus-per-task 20 \
  --slurm-mem-per-cpu 8000
```

Add `--no-skip-plan` if you want the build to run the preflight checks first.

### Memory and threading

There are two different layers to tune:

- `--cores`: total workflow parallelism for the outer Snakemake run
- `default_threads`: per-rule thread count used inside `STAR` genome generation and `bowtie2-build`
- `--slurm-cpus-per-task`: cpus-per-task for the heavy index rules in SLURM mode
- `--slurm-mem` or `--slurm-mem-per-cpu`: scheduler memory request for the heavy index rules in SLURM mode
- `default_ram_bytes`: STAR's internal `--limitGenomeGenerateRAM`
- `bwa_index_mem_mb`: Snakemake memory resource attached to the `bwa_index` rule

Typical patterns:

- 20-core workstation, one large STAR job at a time: set `default_threads: 20` and run `./scripts/bcfngs genomes build --cores 20`
- 20-core workstation, allow two medium jobs in parallel: set `default_threads: 10` and run `./scripts/bcfngs genomes build --cores 20`
- larger-memory node for STAR genome generation: increase `default_ram_bytes`

For a permanent STAR memory change, edit:

```yaml
default_ram_bytes: 250000000000
```

## Rebuild After a Genome Release Change

After [`check-updates`](#check-updates) reports a newer Ensembl release:

1. Edit [`configs/genomes.yaml`](configs/genomes.yaml).
2. Bump `releases.ensembl`, for example `115` to `116`.
3. Review any explicit `fasta_url` or `gtf_url` entries.
4. Run `plan`.
5. Run `build`.

If you want a cautious rollout, start with one organism:

```bash
./scripts/bcfngs genomes build --cores 20 --organism Homo_sapiens
```

## Rebuild After a Tool Version Change

When the genome sequence did not change but an indexer changed, the wrapper build will auto-detect the tool versions from the active environment and use them for the versioned output directories.

If you are using the micromamba environment, update it first:

```bash
./scripts/bcfngs env update
./scripts/bcfngs env check
```

For more detail, see [Environment (micromamba)](environment.html).

If you are using the `modules` profile, load the newer module versions first and then rerun `plan` and `build`.

If you want the checked-in config to document the tool versions explicitly for direct `snakemake` runs, add an `index_versions` block such as:

```yaml
index_versions:
  star: "2.7.11b"
  bwa: "0.7.19-r1273"
  bowtie2: "2.5.5"
```

Then rerun:

```bash
./scripts/bcfngs genomes plan
./scripts/bcfngs genomes build --cores 20
```

Successful builds update the stable symlinks automatically:

- `/fs/pool/pool-bcfngs/genomes/<Organism>/starIndex`
- `/fs/pool/pool-bcfngs/genomes/<Organism>/bwaIndex`
- `/fs/pool/pool-bcfngs/genomes/<Organism>/bowtie2Index`

These stable names point to versioned directories such as `starIndex-2.7.11b`.

## Direct `snakemake` Usage

Use direct `snakemake` when the wrapper does not expose the exact control you need.

### `plan` dry-run

The wrapper does not expose a `--organism` option for `plan`. Keep the direct `snakemake` form when you need a subset dry-run:

```bash
snakemake -npr \
  -s workflow/genomes.smk \
  --configfile configs/genomes.yaml \
  --config org_subset=Homo_sapiens,Mus_musculus
```

This is useful for:

- testing a release bump on one organism first
- checking a new organism block before rebuilding everything
- isolating failures in one species without noise from the rest of the config

For clusters where you want to inspect scheduler-facing settings before a real run:

```bash
snakemake -npr \
  --executor slurm \
  --jobs 20 \
  --default-resources mem_mb=180000 \
  -s workflow/genomes.smk \
  --configfile configs/genomes.yaml
```

`plan` itself does not submit jobs in dry-run mode, but this is still useful to check that your SLURM executor configuration parses correctly.

### `build` targets

The wrapper always targets the full workflow. To build only one tool, target the desired output file directly.

These direct target examples assume the workflow knows which tool versions to use for the versioned output paths. If you run [`workflow/genomes.smk`](workflow/genomes.smk) directly without the wrapper, add an `index_versions` block to [`configs/genomes.yaml`](configs/genomes.yaml) temporarily or inject equivalent values another way.

Only STAR for `Homo_sapiens`:

```bash
snakemake -pr -j 20 \
  -s workflow/genomes.smk \
  --configfile configs/genomes.yaml \
  /fs/pool/pool-bcfngs/genomes/Homo_sapiens/starIndex-<STAR_VERSION>/SA
```

Only bwa for `Homo_sapiens`:

```bash
snakemake -pr -j 20 \
  -s workflow/genomes.smk \
  --configfile configs/genomes.yaml \
  /fs/pool/pool-bcfngs/genomes/Homo_sapiens/bwaIndex-<BWA_VERSION>/Homo_sapiens.bwt
```

Only bowtie2 for `Homo_sapiens`:

```bash
snakemake -pr -j 20 \
  -s workflow/genomes.smk \
  --configfile configs/genomes.yaml \
  /fs/pool/pool-bcfngs/genomes/Homo_sapiens/bowtie2Index-<BOWTIE2_VERSION>/Homo_sapiens.1.bt2
```

These commands still pull in prerequisite downloads, but they avoid rebuilding every index type.
